description: Stream Live IPTV, Video On-Demand, and Series from an Xtream-compatible server using this plugin.
name: Jellyfin Xtream
overview: Stream content from an Xtream-compatible server.

versions.changelog: Fix bundle patch on prod: delete pre-compressed .gz/.br files after patching JS.

## DVR Seekbar for Recording Channels

- Direct HLS seeking with elapsed time clamp
- PlaybackRate lock and live edge buffer
- Bundle patch for correct EPG time display
- Auto-detection of recording channels
Revert to v0.9.76 approach: no getter overrides, proper MediaStreams. iPhone DirectPlays, Android TV copy-mode remux.
Force true DirectPlay: empty MediaStreams + container=hls. Fixes iPhone playback failure.
Force DirectPlay only — disable DirectStream and Transcoding to fix iPhone playback
Counter Normalize() SupportsTranscoding=true override in getter to force DirectPlay
Disable SupportsTranscoding to force DirectPlay of HLS URL — eliminates unnecessary ffmpeg remux on iPhone and Android TV
Probe first segment with ffprobe for real codec metadata (resolution, profile, level, channels) instead of hardcoded values
Add audio channel info (stereo/48kHz) to synthetic streams to prevent unnecessary stereo→5.1 upmix transcode
Disable probing, add synthetic h264+aac MediaStreams to fix Android TV codec detection
Fix Index=-1 from HLS probe to enable DirectStream path
Add getter to counter Normalize() DirectStream override + probing + skip first segment
Skip partial first segment to fix probe failures on channels with missing SPS/PPS
Restore v0.9.54.0 MultiplexedRestream: probing-based, all delivery paths enabled
Enable SupportsTranscoding + AnalyzeDurationMs 3000 for Android startup fix
Revert to v0.9.60.0 MultiplexedRestream — known-good for Android TV DirectStream.

Revert to v0.9.64.0 MultiplexedRestream (HLS playlist approach).

Restores Android TV compatibility. The standard LiveTV stream path experiment (v0.9.65-v0.9.66) is reverted.

Use standard LiveTV stream path for proper AAC BSF support on all clients.

- Replaces custom HLS playlist with LiveTv/LiveStreamFiles endpoint
- Jellyfin automatically adds -bsf:a aac_adtstoasc
- Simplified back to v0.9.54-style MediaSourceInfo
- Works for web, iOS, and Android without transcoding hacks

Fix iOS and web: disable DirectStream to prevent AAC ADTS copy to MP4 without BSF.

Clients now either DirectPlay HLS natively or use HLS transcode path.

Fix web player: enable SupportsTranscoding for HLS remux path.

With SupportsTranscoding=true, the web player can use HLS with video copy + audio transcode, avoiding the AAC ADTS-to-fMP4 issue.
Fix: Use Container=ts so Jellyfin adds aac_adtstoasc bitstream filter when remuxing to fMP4 HLS. Fixes web player playback error (malformed AAC bitstream).
Fix: Increase AnalyzeDurationMs from 500 to 10000 to prevent ffmpeg exit code 234 when remuxing HLS streams. Fixes web player and iOS playback failures.

Two changes to eliminate iOS transcoding:
1. SupportsTranscoding=false — forces DirectStream/DirectPlay
2. Container="mp4" — matches iOS DirectPlayProfile (was "ts" which iOS profiles likely exclude)

Combined with earlier fixes (IsInterlaced=false, SupportsDirectStream=true), this should finally force stream copy on all clients.

Force stream copy on all clients by disabling transcoding.

Swiftfin device profile has conditions that reject H264 for stream copy, forcing H264→AV1 transcode at 0.8x speed (unusable). With SupportsTranscoding=false, the StreamBuilder must choose DirectStream (codec copy) or DirectPlay. H264 via HLS is natively supported on all iOS devices.

Fix iOS/Swiftfin transcoding: Jellyfin Normalize() also sets SupportsDirectStream=false for live TV sources, leaving only DirectPlay or Transcode. Since Swiftfin routes through the server, Transcode was the only option. Now reset SupportsDirectStream=true in MediaSource getter.
Fix iOS/Swiftfin transcoding: remove framerate and lower H264 level to prevent StreamBuilder from choosing AV1 transcode over stream copy.

Fix transcoding on iOS/Swiftfin caused by Jellyfin forcing IsInterlaced=true.

Jellyfin LiveTvMediaSourceProvider.Normalize() unconditionally sets IsInterlaced=true for all non-default LiveTV video streams. This causes iOS/Swiftfin to add a yadif deinterlacer → full H264→AV1 software transcode at ~0.4x realtime.

Fix: MediaSource getter resets IsInterlaced=false each time Jellyfin reads it, undoing the Normalize tampering.

Also adds 9 unit tests for MediaSourceInfo configuration.

Expected: stream copy on all clients (Android TV, iOS, web) — no more transcoding.

Fix transcoding caused by missing codec metadata when probing is disabled.

Changes:
- Provide H264 High + AAC LC MediaStreams with proper Index values
- Jellyfin StreamBuilder can now determine direct play/copy is possible
- Prevents unnecessary H264→HEVC software transcode (was 0.4x realtime)

Combined with v0.9.55.0 (SupportsProbing=false):
- No probe cycle (no open→close→reopen)
- AnalyzeDurationMs=500 preserved (not overridden to 3000)
- Direct play/copy instead of transcode
- Faster startup (~5s vs ~14s)

Fix Android TV startup error by skipping Jellyfin probe cycle.

- Set SupportsProbing=false → eliminates open→probe→close→reopen cycle
- Preserves AnalyzeDurationMs=500 (Jellyfin was overriding to 3000)
- Reduce minSegments from 3 to 2
- Faster poll interval (200ms vs 500ms)

Expected: ~5s faster startup, no more error toast on Android TV.
Persistent ffmpeg via stdin pipe for multiplexer. Eliminates ~4s per-burst overhead in sequential round-robin mode, improving throughput from ~0.6x to ~1.2x for concurrent streams.
